business.headline: Sebi eases trade rules for foreign investors amid rising market outflowsUntil now, investors had to settle each buy and sell trade separately. This meant higher funding needs and more complex transactions. With net settlement, they can offset buy and sell positions, reducing the amount of money required for each cycle.
Oil prices fall over 13% as Trump pauses Iran energy strikesOil prices jumped in the morning with Brent nearing $113, but later fell to around $100.34, down 6.2%, later in the day.

CoinDCX co-founders in custody over fraud claims, company blames fake operatorsThe case is based on a complaint filed by a 42-year-old insurance advisor. He alleged that he was cheated of Rs 71.6 lakh between August 2025 and March 2026.
Sensex ends 1,836 points lower, Nifty below 22,600; Titan down 7%The S&P BSE Sensex lost 1,836 points to close at 72,696.39, while the NSE Nifty50 tanked 601.85 points to end at 22,512.65.

Gold crashes Rs 10,000 in a day, silver down Rs 18,000: Should you buy now?Precious metals have taken a hit, with gold and silver prices falling sharply. While corrections are not unusual, investors are now wondering — is this a buying opportunity or a signal to stay on the sidelines?

Share market crash: Why are IT stocks shining when Dalal Street is in red?A look at Sensex stocks shows that IT names such as HCLTech, Tech Mahindra, and TCS were trading in the green, even as heavyweights across banking, auto, metal and consumption sectors saw sharp declines.

Your iPhone's India-made: Fareed Zakaria's US lecture highlights India pipping ChinaSmartphone shipments from India became its top export while it displaced China as the biggest supplier of iPhones to the US. The feat is being lauded yet again with analyst Fareed Zakaria highlighting it in his lecture. Where does India stand vis a vis China in the smartphones game?

Rupee in free fall: Will it hit Rs 95 against the US dollar?The currency weakened to around Rs 93.9 against the US dollar on Monday, marking its weakest level ever and bringing the Rs 94–95 range into focus.

IndiGo shares hit 52-week low, fall nearly 6%: What is dragging the stock today?IndiGo shares tanked nearly 6% to Rs 3,899.90, down Rs 248 or 5.98%. During the session, it touched a 52-week low of Rs 3,894.80.

China eyes Iranian oil deals after US grants limited sanctions reliefThe US has given a 30-day waiver, allowing some Iranian oil to be sold in order to prevent a sharp jump in global prices. While the relief is temporary, it has opened a small window for buyers, especially in Asia.

Safe haven no more? Why gold, silver prices are crashing despite warGold and silver price are falling even as global tensions rise, turning the usual safe-haven play on its head and catching investors off guard.

HDFC Bank share price falls 4%, hits 52-week low: Why is the stock down?Shares of HDFC Bank were down 3.39% to Rs 754 on the Bombay Stock Exchange at around 10:23 am, extending a sharp decline seen over the past few sessions.

Sensex crashed 1,800 points: 3 reasons why the stock market fell todayAs of 1:10 pm, the S&P BSE Sensex was down 1,923.51 points at 72,609.45, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 625.70 points to 22,488.80. Both indices were down around 2.6–2.7%, reflecting broad-based selling across sectors.

Rupee slides to record low, nears 94 as crude oil surge deepens pressureCrude prices have surged sharply in recent weeks as the conflict in West Asia intensifies. Prices are now hovering above $110 per barrel, a level that immediately hurts countries like India, which import over 80% of their oil needs.

Markets bleed as Hormuz remains on edge; Sensex down 1,500, Rupee at record lowThe Middle East conflict, now entering its fourth week, has intensified after fresh warnings around the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil transit route, raising concerns about potential supply disruptions and a prolonged energy shock.

Brent crude oil hovering above $110 a barrel. Why it spells bad newsMore than three weeks after US and Israeli strikes on Iran triggered a chain of retaliation across the region, the conflict has now spread across West Asia. What started as a geopolitical escalation is now turning into an economic one, and oil is at the centre of it.

HDFC Bank to be in focus today on Dalal Street. Here's all you need to knowThe stock has seen a steep decline recently. In just two sessions, the share price fell from around Rs 843 to Rs 781 on the NSE, marking a drop of over 7%. After closing at Rs 781 on Friday, the stock is now only Rs 11 above its 52-week low of Rs 770.

$53 billion wiped out: Middle East war shakes global airline industryAs the war enters its fourth week, airlines are struggling to cope with a sharp rise in jet fuel prices, which have surged significantly in a short span of time. The impact is already visible for passengers, with ticket prices expected to rise in the coming months as airlines try to protect already thin profit margins.

Oil shock deepens as West Asia war escalates. Is a global recession coming?The world is already feeling the heat as the West Asia conflict enters its fourth week, pushing global crude oil prices higher. In this article, we speak to experts to understand whether this shock could tip the world toward a recession.

Govt nod to hike commercial LPG allocation to 50% as domestic output improvesThe additional allocation will be subject to commercial establishments registering with oil companies and applying for a piped natural gas connection, according to a letter written by the oil secretary to states.

entertainment.headline: Being Bade Sahab: Actor Danish Iqbal shares 9-hour process in makeup without food Danish Iqbal shared he spent up to nine hours daily in prosthetic makeup to transform into 'Bade Sahab' in Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

Tovino Thomas' Pallichattambi teaser promises gritty, hard-hitting period drama A haunting teaser sets the tone for Pallichattambi, as Tovino Thomas leads a gripping tale of resistance against oppression in Kerala's 1950s high ranges.

Dulquer Salmaan, Captain in Prabhas' Kalki 2898 AD, joins Part 2 shoot: Report  With Kalki 2 progressing in Hyderabad, Dulquer Salmaan joins the ongoing schedule, sparking speculation about an expanded role after his cameo in the first film

Real Operation Lyari video of Chaudhary Aslam resurfaces amid Dhurandhar 2 buzz A real-life video of Chaudhary Aslam from Operation Lyari has resurfaced online, coinciding with the buzz around the film Dhurandhar 2 where Sanjay Dutt portrays him.

Lokesh Kanagaraj's AK-47 rifles seized amid poll check. Read what happened next An AK-47 scare during poll checks in Chennai turned out to be a film prop mix-up, as 10 dummy AK-47 rifles linked to Lokesh Kanagaraj's project were seized and later returned after verification.

BTS' ARIRANG comeback breaks records; show why they remain the world's biggest band BTS' return with ARIRANG signalled a powerful comeback and global dominance, as the album delivered 110 million streams in 24 hours. It is projected to cross 5 million units in its first week.

Dhurandhar 2: Madras High Court plea seeks ban on Tamil Nadu release, says report Election code concerns reportedly hit Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge in Tamil Nadu as Madras High Court hears plea seeking a ban ahead of state elections.

Dhurandhar 2 scene with cameraman in mirror: Tribute to The Dark Knight or gaffe? A viral moment in Dhurandhar: The Revenge had fans debating whether a visible cameraman in a key scene was a clever tribute or a major oversight. Here's why the internet was divided.

Anushka Sharma to join Allu Arjun and Deepika Padukone in Atlee's AA22xA6: Report Actor Anushka Sharma is in talks to join Allu Arjun-Deepika Padukone's upcoming sci-fi film A22xA6, according to reports. The film is directed by Atlee and aims for a visual scale and world-building comparable to the Avatar franchise.

Dhurandhar 2 reduces Dawood Ibrahim to footnote: Real peak detailing by Aditya Dhar In the middle of its aggression and calculated drama, Dhurandhar: The Revenge turns its portrayal of Dawood Ibrahim into a masterstroke. Aditya Dhar builds his world, only to deliberately dismantle it when it comes to the once-feared gangster.

Dhurandhar 2: Singing with Satinder Sartaaj a dream come true for Jasmin Sandlas Punjabi singer Jasmin Sandlas called her collaboration with Satinder Sartaaj on Jaiye Sajana a dream come true, praising the music and film team for their guidance. The track from Dhurandhar: The Revenge has gone viral.

Rakesh Bedi urges fans not to share Dhurandhar 2 spoilers: Let others enjoy the film Actor Rakesh Bedi has urged fans not to share spoilers of Dhurandhar 2. He emphasised the significance of preserving the movie experience and requested viewers to avoid filming scenes, especially the climax, during screenings.

Dhurandhar 2: Meet Udaybir Sandhu, actor who plays Jaskirat's friend Pinda In Dhurandhar 2, actor Udaybir Sandhu plays Jaskirat Singh, aka Pinda, Ranveer Singh's on-screen childhood friend. Here's everything you need to know about the actor's journey from being a national-level hockey player to working in Bollywood.

Mazaak nahi: Makeup artist recalls Ranveer's strict approach on Dhurandhar 2 set Dhurandhar: The Revenge makeup artist Karandeep Singh revealed how Ranveer Singh handled intense transformation for the film. He appreciated Ranveer's dedication despite heavy prosthetics during the shoot.

Ranveer's Dhurandhar beats Jawan, Pathaan, Animal at box office in one weekend Dhurandhar: The Revenge is only a weekend old, and it has already shattered the biggest records set by the previous Hindi blockbusters like Jawan, Pathaan and Animal. Here's a detailed look at its records, and the comparison with other biggies.

Dhurandhar 2's Uzair, Danish Pandor, calls Ranveer one of the finest actors Danish Pandor, who plays Uzair Baloch in Dhurandhar: The Revenge, praises Ranveer Singh as one of the finest actors of this generation, highlighting his irreplaceable contribution to the film.

I'm still processing it: Arjun Rampal says he's humbled by Dhurandhar 2 response Arjun Rampal, who plays Major Iqbal in Dhurandhar 2, said he is still processing the response to the film. The actor credited the film's team for shaping his villainous role of ISI chief.

Weak detailing in Dhurandhar 2? Fans say they spot editing gaffes in Ranveer film Dhurandhar: The Revenge continues to dominate the box office while drawing attention for a few noticeable on-screen errors. Viral clips of these slip-ups have sparked conversations online.

Meet Mustafa Ahmed, Ranveer Singh's trainer who played spy Rizwan in Dhurandhar From gym floors to film sets, Mustafa Ahmed's resilience defines his unique path in Bollywood. He plays Rizwan in Ranveer Singh-starrer Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

general news.headline: Trump halts promised Iran strikes after 'productive talks'; Tehran disputes claim Live updatesTrump on Monday paused strikes on Iranian energy sites for five days after "productive talks". Tehran, however, said the move followed its warning of wider regional retaliation.

4 killed as cold storage facility collapses in UP's Prayagraj, ammonia leak reportedFollowing the collapse, ammonia gas began leaking from the facility, prompting emergency containment measures. Officials said efforts are underway to secure the area and prevent further risk.

India braced for energy shock, PM warns of tough days aheadPM Modi said India, with a 53 lakh MT strategic petroleum reserve and energy imports from 41 countries, is well-prepared to meet its needs despite rising West Asia tensions and concerns over disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

NDA seals Tamil Nadu seat deal: AIADMK to contest on 178, BJP 27 out of 234The NDA has finalised Tamil Nadu seat sharing with AIADMK contesting 178 seats and BJP 27, while PMK and AMMK also get shares.

Dhurandhar 2 reduces Dawood Ibrahim to footnote: Real peak detailing by Aditya DharIn the middle of its aggression and calculated drama, Dhurandhar: The Revenge turns its portrayal of Dawood Ibrahim into a masterstroke. Aditya Dhar builds his world, only to deliberately dismantle it when it comes to the once-feared gangster.
Newark airport's control tower evacuated after burning smell, flight ops hitNewark airport control tower evacuated over burning smell hours after plane collision, flight ops halted again

Rs 200 crore bungalow undisclosed: Congress seeks action against Kerala BJP chiefThe party cited property tax records and his previous affidavit to back its claim, urging the Election Commission to act under the Representation of the People Act. Chandrasekhar, who has declared assets exceeding Rs 93 crore, is yet to respond to the allegations.

Armed group warns foreign players to skip PSL, says Pakistan not safe for sportsAn armed group has warned overseas players to skip the 2026 edition of Pakistan Super League, citing a volatile security situation and calling Pakistan unfit for peaceful sporting activity.

Real Operation Lyari video of Chaudhary Aslam resurfaces amid Dhurandhar 2 buzzA real-life video of Chaudhary Aslam from Operation Lyari has resurfaced online, coinciding with the buzz around the film Dhurandhar 2 where Sanjay Dutt portrays him.

Live: Closely following developments, says government on Trump's Iran retreatUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Middle East conflict has entered its fourth week, with tensions showing tentative signs of easing alongside continued military posturing. US President Donald Trump has announced a five-day pause on planned strikes targeting Iranian power and energy infrastructure, citing "very good and productive" talks with Tehran aimed at a complete resolution of hostilities. Even as diplomatic engagement gains momentum, concerns persist over the security of the Strait of Hormuz, with multiple countries urging safe passage for commercial vessels. Iran, meanwhile, has rejected claims of charging ships for transit through the strategic waterway as global attention remains fixed on the region's economic and security implications. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the Middle East crisis.

For Ravindra Kaushik, India's real Dhurandhar in Pakistan, it didn't end wellIndia's original Dhurandhar, Ravindra Kaushik, rose from acting at college theatres, to infiltrating the Pakistan Army as a RAW Agent. He provided critical intelligence on Pakistani troop movements and the country's nuclear programme, but died a lonely death after his betrayal and subsequent capture by the ISI.

Shias can go to Iran: Pak clerics furious over Army Chief Munir's warningPakistan Army chief Asim Munir said Shias who "love Iran so much" should "go to Iran". His remarks triggered a row with prominent Shia clerics of Pakistan accusing Munir of insulting the community and the military establishment of serving America and Israel as a "servant".

Ageing Germany looks to India to fill job slots, from healthcare to IT to plumbingEurope's largest economy, Germany, has been dealing with a persistent shortage of skilled workers in sectors like healthcare and IT, among others. The crisis is driven by a decline in native Germans entering the workforce. Employers are now looking to plug the gaps with skilled workers from India.
Oil prices fall over 13% as Trump pauses Iran energy strikesOil prices jumped in the morning with Brent nearing $113, but later fell to around $100.34, down 6.2%, later in the day.

Salesforce CEO says engineers no longer required as AI takes overSalesforce CEO says AI agents have boosted productivity so much that hiring new engineers or service staff wasn't needed this year. While AI handles millions in tasks, humans continue to play a key role in the company's operations.
Delhi power bills may spike as government moves to clear Rs 38,552 crore duesFrom April 2026, the Delhi government is planning to recover over Rs 38,000 crore in dues to discoms.

Small is big in Tamil Nadu as unwieldy alliances make electoral chemistry a challengeAs Tamil Nadu heads into a high-stakes 2026 election, old allies have turned into rivals fighting deeply personal political battles. With splintered loyalties and shifting alliances, even marginal vote shifts could tilt the balance in a tightly contested race.

Dhurandhar 2 hurtles towards a historic Rs 1,000 crore in Week 1 itselfRanveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has wrapped up its first weekend with an impressive worldwide total. The spy thriller is now eyeing Chhaava and Stree 2, while also targeting entry into the Rs 1,000 crore club within its first week.

Gold crashes Rs 10,000 in a day, silver down Rs 18,000: Should you buy now?Precious metals have taken a hit, with gold and silver prices falling sharply. While corrections are not unusual, investors are now wondering — is this a buying opportunity or a signal to stay on the sidelines?

Nashik godman's dark deeds: Rape, impregnation, forced abortion, murder for landWhat began as an abuse complaint has snowballed into a massive scandal involving rape allegations, hidden camera evidence, fraud and even murder claims, as more victims come forward against a Nashik godman.

Masters of camouflage: How many owls can you spot in this picture?Perfectly blending into their surroundings, these birds demonstrate camouflage so effective that even trained eyes can miss them at first.

China is rewriting classrooms for the AI age; India is still chasing marksChina is beginning to train students for the demands of a changing world, with a growing focus on skills and adaptability. India, meanwhile, remains rooted in a system where marks still decide opportunity. The gap between the two is no longer theoretical, it is starting to show.

Weak detailing in Dhurandhar 2? Fans say they spot editing gaffes in Ranveer filmDhurandhar: The Revenge continues to dominate the box office while drawing attention for a few noticeable on-screen errors. Viral clips of these slip-ups have sparked conversations online.

What is the 'no-spend' challenge and why is it trendingIn a world that's always looking for a digital detox, this TikTok trend asks you to go for a financial detox. Hold your judgments and eye rolls, because it might just be useful, given the current circumstances.

sports.headline: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is not professional: Jitesh Sharma lifts lid on prodigy IPL 2026: Jitesh Sharma says Sooryavanshi is 'not professional' off the field. The reason? He can't stop eating ice cream at night. Cricket's brightest teen is just being a teenager.

PSL loses another star as Rajasthan Royals confirm Sam Curran's replacement IPL 2026: Dasun Shanaka replaced injured Sam Curran in Rajasthan Royals' IPL 2026 squad. The Sri Lankan all-rounder joined the franchise for Rs 2 crore after ditching PSL.

IPL 2026: Delhi Capitals await Mitchell Starc's NOC from Cricket Australia IPL 2026: Delhi Capitals are awaiting Cricket Australia's NOC for Mitchell Starc. The franchise remains hopeful he will join before their IPL 2026 opener.

Gavaskar blasts IPL overseas stars, urges teams to drop players for last-minute exit IPL 2026: Sunil Gavaskar has called on IPL franchises to drop overseas players who pull out of tournament at the last minute, saying some are taking advantage of India's tradition of hospitality.

Hardik Pandya urged to step down as Mumbai captain ahead of new IPL season IPL 2026: Former India opener Kris Srikkanth has urged Hardik Pandya to relinquish the captaincy, saying Suryakumar Yadav, fresh from T20 World Cup success, should lead Mumbai Indians. Hardik has led MI since returning from Gujarat Titans in 2024.

Koneru Humpy withdraws from Candidates Tournament citing security concerns India's star chess player Koneru Humpy has withdrawn from the FIDE Women's Candidates Tournament. Humpy's decision comes after she lashed out at the organisers over safety concerns in warn-torn West Asia.

KL Rahul will open in IPL 2026, confirms Delhi Capitals captain Axar Patel Senior batter KL Rahul will open the innings for Delhi Capitals, revealed the team management ahead of the team's IPL opener. Last season, Delhi played with an array of opening combinations, resulting in inconsistent performances.

Armed group warns foreign players to skip PSL, says Pakistan not safe for sports An armed group has warned overseas players to skip the 2026 edition of Pakistan Super League, citing a volatile security situation and calling Pakistan unfit for peaceful sporting activity.

Dhoni shares wholesome moment with Sarfaraz Khan's son, video goes viral A heartwarming video of MS Dhoni and Sarfaraz Khan's son has gone viral. In the video, the CSK legend can be seen playing with Sarfaraz's one-year-old son.

New Zealand Cricket director resigns after NZ20 replaces iconic Super Smash New Zealand Cricket has backed a new NZ20 franchise league to replace the Super Smash. Hours after the decision was made official, board director Dion Nash issued his resignation.

Virat Kohli hits rapid 29, Tim David, Venkatesh Iyer bat well in RCB practice Royal Challengers Bengaluru held their first practice game on Sunday, March 22, ahead of the Indian Premier League 2026 season. Their batters came off the blocks quickly and batted with aggressive intent.

Petrol Shortage League: Fans troll PSL over oil crisis stepsPakistan Super League is being trolled by cricket fans after the league decided to trim down its operations. The reaction comes after PCB head Mohsin Naqvi announced that the league will held in only 2 cities this season. That too, behind closed doors.
Messi scores 901st career goal as Inter Miami rally past New YorkLionel Messi scored his 901st career goal as Inter Miami came from behind to beat New York City FC 3-2 in a thrilling MLS clash.

Real Madrid beat Atletico in pulsating derby, stay alive in LaLiga raceReal Madrid vs Atletico Madrid: Vinicius Jr scored twice as Real Madrid came from behind to beat Atletico Madrid 3-2 in a thrilling derby, keeping their LaLiga title push firmly on track.

Carlos Alcaraz stunned by world No. 36 Sebastian Korda in Miami OpenMiami Open: World no.1 Carlos Alcaraz has been stunned by United States Sebastian Korda in the third round of the Miami Open. Korda, ranked 36th in the world, bested Alcaraz in three sets.

Hardik Pandya's MI goals in 2026: Win 6th title, build legacyMumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya wants to win the 6th Indian Premier League title for the franchise in IPL 2026. In an interview, Hardik said that it was important to him that MI played their best-ever cricket this season.

PSL's loss is IPL's gain: How many players have quit Pakistan league so far?The PSL's overlap with the IPL over the last two seasons has created a growing problem for the PCB, with players increasingly leaving the Pakistan Super League for lucrative IPL replacement deals.

Arsenal's ship springs first hole as Man City snatch Carabao Cup from ArtetaArsenal's push for a historic season hit its first roadblock as Manchester City punished their mistakes in the Carabao Cup final. With bigger prizes still in sight, Arteta's side must respond quickly to avoid this becoming a familiar pattern again.

How World Cup win changed women's cricket in India: Jemimah Rodrigues explainsIn an interview with Sports Today, Jemimah Rodrigues said India's 2025 Women's World Cup win has inspired a new generation of young cricketers and boosted recognition for female players.

Suresh Raina, Matthew Hayden inducted into CSK's first-ever Hall of FameIPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings have inducted legends Suresh Raina and Matthew Hayden into their first-ever Hall of Fame, honouring their outstanding contributions to the franchise.

Virat Kohli reminisces RCB's deserving IPL 2025 triumph: It was not a flukeIPL 2026: Virat Kohli said RCB's IPL 2025 title win was no fluke. He highlighted the team's confidence and consistency throughout the campaign.

MS Dhoni asked to play till 60, chat with actor Sivakarthikeyan goes viralIPL 2026: MS Dhoni joked about playing IPL till he is 60-year-old during a CSK fan event. His response to actor Sivakarthikeyan sparked fresh excitement among fans.

PCB will take action against players opting out of PSL for IPL: Mohsin NaqviPCB chief Mohsin Naqvi has warned that players who leave the PSL for the IPL will face action under league rules, citing Corbin Bosch's one-year ban last year as a precedent.

Watch: Suresh Raina rolls back years, hits Noor Ahmed for six in front of MS DhoniIPL 2026: Suresh Raina thrilled Chepauk with a vintage six off Noor Ahmed. The moment, with MS Dhoni watching on, sent fans into nostalgia mode.

Rishabh Pant with most pressure in IPL 2026: Du Plessis urges LSG captain to deliverIPL 2026: Faf du Plessis believes Rishabh Pant will face the most pressure in IPL 2026. The LSG captain will need to deliver after a disappointing 2025 season.

MS Dhoni steals spotlight at CSK reunion, honours Sanju Samson, Shivam DubeMS Dhoni stole the spotlight at CSK's 'Roar 26' event in Chennai, as former stars, including Suresh Raina and Matthew Hayden, reunited at the MA Chidambaram Stadium on Sunday. The former captain also honoured World Cup-winning stars Sanju Samson, Shivam Dube and Ayush Mhatre ahead of IPL 2026.

Thatrom Thookrom: Sanju Samson thrills CSK fans with Tamil punchlineIPL 2026: Sanju Samson thrilled CSK fans with a Tamil punchline at the ROAR 2026 event. His words and presence sparked massive cheers at Chepauk.

Australia Women shrug off India setback to hammer West Indies in T20I seriesAUSW vs WIW, 2nd T20I: Australia Women sealed the T20I series against West Indies with a 17-run win in the second match in Kingstown, bouncing back strongly after last month's 2-1 home series defeat to India and taking an unassailable 2-0 lead.

PSL continues to lose players as Shanaka set to join IPL as injury replacementIPL 2026: Dasun Shanaka is set to leave PSL side Lahore Qalandars to join Rajasthan Royals as Sam Curran's replacement. His move adds to the growing list of players choosing IPL over PSL.

KKR fans furious as bowling coach Bravo spotted at CSK event during injury crisisIPL 2026: KKR fans erupted in anger after bowling coach Dwayne Bravo was spotted at CSK's event, raising questions about divided loyalties amid a mounting injury crisis at the franchise.

South Africa hold back New Zealand in Wellington to force T20I series deciderSouth Africa beat New Zealand by 19 runs in the fourth T20I in Wellington to level the five-match series at 2-2 and force a decider, after posting 164 for five and bowling the hosts out for 145 in 18.5 overs.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
